blocks.text: How long, O Lord? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?
Ps 13: How long will the enemy threaten my life? An individual petition; vv. 1–2 are the complaint; vv. 3–4, the petition, and vv. 5–6, the statement of trust and promise of praise
1 : How long? Ancient sufferers sought to know exactly how long they must suffer; see Pss 35.17; 74.9–10; Isa 6.11. , God has refused to look kindly at the psalmist.
How long must I bear pain in my soul, and have sorrow in my heart all day long? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
Consider and answer me, O Lord my God! Give light to my eyes, or I will sleep the sleep of death,
3 : , a contemporary paraphrase might be, “Look at me! Talk to me!” referring back to the complaint in v. 1 that the has hidden his face. , i.e., give me joy and vitality (see Prov 29.13).
and my enemy will say, “I have prevailed”; my foes will rejoice because I am shaken.
But I trusted in your steadfast love; my heart shall rejoice in your salvation.
5 : , better, “trust.” Anxiety gives way to trust as is typical of individual petitions.
I will sing to the Lord, because he has dealt bountifully with me.
